rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTINE YOUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Filing A Written Report Of The Estate Bank Account
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) Letters have not been issued. (2) A written and verified report of the estate bank account has not been filed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STELLA STAVROS KINGAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/25. (No earlier availability.) (1) The ex parte petition to increase bond was not filed until 12/17/24 (despite the allegation in paragraph 4 of the second amended petition filed on 11/27/24 and requirements of CRC 7.204), and a copy of it was apparently not left with the clerk for delivery to the director for assignment to an examiner, as required by LRSF 14.11 B. The ex parte petition is not on the court's tracking sheet as having been logged in and assigned. (2) The administrator sold assets after the end of the accounting period (7/31/24), which must be shown in an accounting. (3) Losses on sale must be included in the fee calculation. (4) If the post-deceased nephews' heirs are their successors-in-interest, and the distribution to those nephews is less than the amount set forth in Probate Code Section 13100, it might not be necessary to open formal estates for them... See LRSF 14.25 G. (5) Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters. [LRSF 14.34 D.] (6) The E-Trade statement showed 549 shares of Paramount, not 649 shares. (7) There appear to be duplicate payments on Schedule H (liabilities) for payments to Alaska Airlines on 1/14/23 ($334.80 x 2), again on 1/14/23 ($238.20 x 2), $3/1/23 ($272.97 x 2), 5/3/23 ($148.98 x2). Please explain. (8) What were the USPS, UPS Store, and Postal Annex charges for? (Not all payments for which Administrator reimbursed herself are adequately described.) (9) Probate Code Section 11420 concerns just the priority for payment of estate debts and expenses, not the division of fees and cost attributable to each heir's share. Probate Code Sections 21402 and 21403 concern abatement of shares to beneficiaries, which is not at issue here. (10) It seems unlikely that the tangible personal property was sold for exactly the appraised value. Proof might need to be filed from whoever oversaw the sale of those assets. Please provide a (revised) proposed order and courtesy copies of the amendment to the second amended petition (or third amended petition), proof of service, and any other papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/26/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE GENERAL DURABLE POWER OF ATTORNEY OF HELGA DOONAN DATED JULY 1, 2024
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION Petition To Affirm Petitioner'S Status As Attorney-In-Fact Under Power Of Attorney; To Compel Third Party To Honor Authority Of Attorney-In-Fact
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/10/25 due to Attorney's unavailabiltiy until 1/6/25, the jiudicial officer's unavailability and holiday in January, and the examiner's unavailability in February. Issues to be addressed: 1) When a petition seeks to compel a third person to honor the authority of an attorney-in-fact, notice with a copy of the petition must be served on the third person in the manner provided in CCP Section 413.10. No such proof of service of summons has been filed. 2) The petition does not disclose any information about the petitioner's relationship to the principal or why the durable power of attorney was executed so close to the date that the principal was deemed to be incapacitated, which could help the Court. (The document does not appear to have been completed by Helga Doonan, just initialed and signed by her.) 3) It is unclear why the Court should order all of the funds on deposit with the bank to be delivered to Petitioner. If the durable power of attorney is deemed to be valid, the attorney-in-fact will have authority to manage the principal's accounts, etc. Deadline is 2/26/25 to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any new filed papers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WESSON FAMILY TRUST, DATED JANUARY 1, 2020
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For An Order Confirming The Settlement Agreement Executed On September 30, 2024, Following Mediation
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/12/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Notice must be given directly to a person entitled to notice, not in care of another. If a party entitled to notice is represented by an attorney, notice must also be given to the attorney, but not only to the attorney. [CRC 7.51(a) and (b)]. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/26/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FLORENCE V. TASSANO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/3/2025. The following issues to be cleared two weeks before the hearing: (1) Proof of publication. (2) Written declination to act or consent from the decedent's daughter to the proposed administrator's appointment. Local rule 14.24. (3) Proposed order to be submitted. Local Rule 14.7.A. (4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [Form DE-147S] should not be attached to DE-147 as the form instructs. Local Rule 14.18.E. (5) Proof of service of the notice must be dated by the server in the signature line. (6) Proof of service of Notice of Directions for Remote Appearances. Local Rule 14.1.C.6) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
